items.question: For one-hot true class y, what is H(P, Q)?
items.explanation: Only the true class contributes: H(P,Q) = −p(y) log q(y) = −log q(y) when p(y)=1.

items.question: If P = Q, then H(P, Q) equals:
items.explanation: Identical distributions give H(P,Q) = −Σ p log p = H(P).

items.question: Fair coin P, model q(H)=0.5, q(T)=0.5. H(P,Q) in bits is:
items.explanation: P = Q here, so H(P,Q) = H(P) = 1 bit for a fair coin.

items.question: D_KL(P || Q) equals:
items.explanation: Cross-entropy decomposes into entropy plus KL excess.

items.question: Which framework input is correct for nn.CrossEntropyLoss?
items.explanation: CrossEntropyLoss expects unnormalized logits and class indices; it applies log-softmax internally.
